subject: Sociology
title: Reparations Debate
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Reparations Debate.

hook.text: Germany paid reparations for the Holocaust. Japan compensated interned Americans. Black Americans are still waiting.

buildup.text: Reparations means compensation for historical injustice. For Black Americans, that includes slavery and Jim Crow.

discovery.text: Ta-Nehisi Coates argued that reparations address not just slavery but 150 years of policy theft after emancipation.

twist.text: Opposition focuses on logistics: who pays, who receives, how much? Supporters say those are solvable policy details.

climax.text: Reparations aren't charity. They're repayment for documented theft: stolen labor, denied loans, seized property.

punchline.text: The debt is documented. The question is whether to pay it.

quiz.question: What do reparations aim to address?
options: Compensation for slavery and post-slavery policy theft
Charitable donations to all low-income people
